query: Which cases invoke Section 302 of the Indian Penal Code?
note: graph/structured — clean section_key match expected, no known act-name variant issue here

query: Which cases invoke Article 374 of the Constitution of India?
note: graph/structured — small clean slice, good sanity check

query: Which cases involve Section 66 of the Indian Income-tax Act?
note: graph/structured

query: Which cases were decided under the Representation of the People Act?
note: graph/structured

query: Which cases invoke the Criminal Procedure Code?
note: BUG TEST — true set spans two stored variants: 'Criminal Procedure Code' (6 cases: 1950_10, 1950_28, 1953_31, 1953_78, 1953_88, 1956_11) and 'Code of Criminal Procedure' (4 cases: 1952_21, 1952_93, 2008_1460, 2010_721). The fuzzy CONTAINS match in get_cases_by_act won't unify these — expect ~half the true set depending on which phrasing the classifier extracts from the query.

query: Which cases invoke the Civil Procedure Code?
note: BUG TEST — same fragmentation as q5, civil side: 'Civil Procedure Code' (3 cases) vs 'Code of Civil Procedure' (5 cases) vs 'Code of Civil Procedure, 1908' (1 case, 2008_188)

query: Which cases did Justice Mehr Chand Mahajan preside over?
note: BUG TEST — 1952_51 is stored as judge name 'MAHAJANJ' (OCR-garbled 'Mahajan, J.'), not 'Mehr Chand Mahajan'. Exact-match judge lookup will miss it. This is the bug already flagged in your README's Known Limitations, now with a concrete failing case.

query: Which cases did Justice Patanjali Sastri preside over?
note: control — spelling is consistent across all occurrences, should retrieve cleanly. Compare against q7 to isolate 'exact-match works when extraction is consistent' vs 'fails when it isn't'

query: What happened in case 2019_890?
note: by_case_id control — single exact lookup

query: Summarize case 2002_151
note: by_case_id control

query: What cases does 2019_890 cite?
note: DIAGNOSTIC, not a recall test — none of 2019_890's 9 cited IDs exist in this 71-case corpus, so get_cited_cases will correctly return empty. Expected result is 0 results, not a failure. Exclude from quantitative averages; use to document the 'citation graph is sparse at this scale' limitation.

query: What cases does 1953_88 cite?
note: DIAGNOSTIC — same purpose as q11, second data point. Checked all 71 cases' cites lists against each other: zero intra-corpus citation overlaps exist anywhere in this set.

query: Cases involving murder convictions and appeals against conviction under the IPC
note: semantic/vector — identical ground truth to q1, deliberately, to compare vector recall vs graph recall on the same true set

query: Cases about income tax assessment and reference appeals
note: semantic/vector

query: Cases about preventive detention and habeas corpus petitions
note: semantic/vector

query: Cases about freedom of speech, press, and restrictions on public order
note: semantic/vector — these three look like the classic early press-freedom trio (Madras/East Punjab public order + Press Emergency Powers Act cases)

query: Cases about challenges to arbitration awards or an arbitrator's mandate
note: semantic/vector

query: Cases about election petitions and disputes
note: semantic/vector — identical ground truth to q4, compare vector vs graph

query: Cases about tenancy, leases, and rent control disputes
note: semantic/vector

query: Cases about Hindu personal law, succession, and inheritance
note: semantic/vector — 1953_106 has no usable sections extracted ('section not specified'), so this case can ONLY be found via vector search, never graph. Good isolation test.

query: Cases about Muslim personal law and dower (mahr)
note: semantic/vector — single-case precision test

query: Cases about Andhra Pradesh local cadre recruitment disputes under Article 371-D
note: DATA QUALITY FLAG, not just a retrieval test — 1947_378 and 2014_170 have near-identical snippets, the same judge (S. Thakur), and overlapping acts/sections/citations. They may be the same judgment ingested under two different dataset IDs. Worth manually checking the raw HF dataset entries before trusting this as two distinct cases.

query: What happened in case 2007_946?
note: EXTRACTION QUALITY FLAG — this case's extracted 'judges' field includes Cardozo, Frankfurter, Holmes, and Hugo Black, who are US Supreme Court justices, never Indian SC judges. Almost certainly pulled from a citation inside the judgment text rather than the actual bench. Good evidence for an entity-extraction-accuracy metric.

query: What happened in case 1954_15?
note: EXTRACTION QUALITY FLAG — this case has zero judges, acts, or sections extracted despite having a full text snippet. A clean example of a total extraction failure for the entity-extraction accuracy writeup.
